description: Treatment Room 3 won't hold temperature. The thermostat is set to 70 but the room is reading in the low 60s by mid-morning, which is too cold for patients during manual therapy. Other rooms on the same wing seem fine.
short_description: Treatment Room 3 HVAC running too cold

description: The Chattanooga ultrasound therapy unit in the modalities bay is throwing an error and won't deliver output. The head powers on but the screen shows a fault code and the timer won't start. We've had to move patients to the second unit.
short_description: Ultrasound therapy unit fault code, no output

description: The hydraulic exam table in Treatment Room 1 won't stay raised. It slowly drifts down on its own when a patient is on it, which is a safety concern. Foot pedal seems to work but pressure isn't holding.
short_description: Treatment table hydraulics failing in Room 1

description: WiFi is completely down in the PT gym / rehab floor. Therapists can't pull up exercise programs on the tablets and the music system tied to the network is offline. The front office network appears unaffected.
short_description: WiFi down across the PT gym

description: The EMR workstation at the Room 2 charting station won't boot. It powers on, shows the manufacturer logo, then sits on a black screen. Tried a hard restart twice with no luck. Clinicians are charting on paper for now.
The badge-access reader on the staff/clinical-area door isn't reading badges. Staff have to be let in manually from the front desk. The LED stays red and there's no beep when a badge is tapped.
short_description: Badge-access door reader not reading badges

description: The autoclave/sterilizer in the back is alarming and showing a fault on the cycle. It won't complete a sterilization run. We need this cleared before we can process instruments again.
short_description: Autoclave sterilizer alarm, cycle won't complete

description: The front-desk printer is offline and won't pick up print jobs. We can't print visit summaries or superbills for patients checking out. Power-cycling the printer didn't bring it back online.
The VoIP phone system is dropping calls mid-conversation, especially at the front desk. Patients calling to schedule are getting cut off. It's been intermittent all morning.

The ice machine we use for cold-therapy packs is leaking water onto the floor in the supply alcove. There's a steady puddle forming and I've put towels down for now. Needs to be looked at before it spreads.

Several of the exterior lights in the back parking lot are out. The lot is dark for staff and patients leaving after evening appointments. Looks like at least three pole fixtures are not coming on at dusk.
The wall outlet that powers equipment in Treatment Room 4 is dead. Nothing plugged into either receptacle gets power. The traction unit and lamp won't turn on, so the room is out of service.
The label printer at the front desk that prints chart labels keeps jamming on every other label. The feed misaligns and we have to clear it constantly. Slowing down patient intake.
The server room temperature alarm is going off. The wall thermostat in there is reading higher than normal and the cooling doesn't seem to be keeping up. Flagging before equipment overheats.
The guest WiFi captive portal isn't loading for patients in the waiting area. The 'Iron City Guest' network shows up but the sign-in page never appears, so guests can't get online while they wait.
short_description: Guest WiFi captive portal not loading

description: Our e-fax line is down. Referrals and physician orders that normally come in by fax aren't arriving, and outbound faxes to referring offices are failing. Need this restored, it affects patient care.
short_description: E-fax line down, referrals not arriving

description: The patient self check-in kiosk in the lobby is frozen on a white screen and won't accept taps. Patients are lining up at the desk to check in manually. A reboot didn't fix it.
The treadmill in the rehab gym is showing an error code and stops after about 30 seconds of use. We use it for return-to-run testing, so it being down affects several patients this week.
There's a water leak in the back supply room, looks like it's coming from the ceiling near the corner. Some of the boxed supplies on the top shelf are getting wet. Putting a bucket under it for now.

The clinical tablets in the gym keep dropping their connection to the EMR mid-session and we have to log back in. It happens most often near the far end of the gym. Disrupts documenting exercises in real time.
short_description: Clinical tablets dropping EMR session in gym
